steps.title: Find the explicit point of disagreement
steps.content: Text 1 says the moon formed from debris "over the course of thousands of years." Text 2 says formation "happened immediately after impact, taking just a few hours." The contrast is entirely about speed of formation.

steps.title: Match that contrast to a choice
steps.content: The correct answer should describe Text 1 as viewing formation as slower and Text 2 as viewing it as faster.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'formation...occurred much earlier': overreach — neither text dates when the collision happened; they disagree on duration, not date. ✗
  • Choice B — 'more evidence confirming the existence of Theia': Theia's existence is not disputed in either text. ✗
  • Choice C — 'moon's surface is more similar to Earth's surface': neither text discusses surface composition or similarity. ✗
  • Choice D — 'moon formed more slowly': directly captures the thousands-of-years vs. hours contrast. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
